sources.citation: James et al. ISLR 2nd ed. Springer, 2021.
Stanford CS229 Machine Learning, Andrew Ng, 2022.

learning_objectives: L1: Define supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning with one example each.
L1: Identify bias, variance, overfitting, and underfitting.
L1: State the purpose of a train/validation/test split.
L2: Explain how gradient descent minimises a loss function.
L2: Describe how a decision tree makes predictions and how random forests improve on single trees.
L2: Explain what cross-validation measures and why it is preferred over a single train-test split.
L3: Diagnose overfitting or underfitting from learning curves and prescribe a remedy.
L3: Implement a complete ML pipeline: load, split, train, evaluate, interpret.

concepts.title: Regression, Classification, Evaluation
concepts.explanation: Supervised: learn f(X)→y from labelled examples. Regression: y continuous. Classification: y categorical. Metrics — Regression: RMSE, R². Classification: accuracy, precision=TP/(TP+FP), recall=TP/(TP+FN), F1=harmonic mean, AUC-ROC. Use F1 for imbalanced classes.
concepts.worked_example: Spam with 95% ham: accuracy=95% predicting all ham. F1=0. Use precision-recall curve instead.

concepts.title: Train/Validation/Test Split
concepts.explanation: Train: fit parameters. Validation: tune hyperparameters. Test: final unbiased estimate — never touch until tuning complete. Data leakage: fit scaler before split contaminates test set. Fix: use sklearn Pipeline.
concepts.worked_example: Wrong: fit scaler on all data → split. Right: split first → fit scaler on train → transform both.

concepts.title: Gradient Descent and Variants
concepts.explanation: Update: w ← w − α·∂L/∂w. Batch GD: full dataset. SGD: one sample. Mini-batch: 32–256 samples. Adam: adaptive per-parameter rates — most used in deep learning.
concepts.worked_example: Linear regression MSE: α=0.01 converges ~1000 steps. α=1.5 oscillates/diverges.

concepts.title: Bias-Variance Trade-off
concepts.explanation: MSE = Bias² + Variance + Irreducible noise. High bias = underfitting. High variance = overfitting. L2 Ridge: adds λΣw². L1 Lasso: sparse/feature selection. K-fold CV: more reliable than single split.
concepts.worked_example: Train low, val high and diverging → overfit → more data, dropout, regularisation. Both high → underfit → more features or complex model.

concepts.title: Decision Trees and Random Forests
concepts.explanation: Decision tree: high variance. Random forest: N trees on bootstrap samples, average predictions — reduces variance. XGBoost: sequential trees on residuals — often best on tabular data.
concepts.worked_example: Credit risk: 100-tree forest, majority vote. Feature importance: debt-to-income and credit score dominate.

concepts.title: K-Means and PCA
concepts.explanation: K-means: assign to nearest centroid, recompute, repeat. Choose k via elbow/silhouette. PCA: orthogonal directions of max variance for dimensionality reduction.
concepts.worked_example: Customer segmentation: k=4 (elbow). Four segments: occasional high-value, frequent low-value, VIP, at-risk.
